message:
fix(installer): add --recurse-submodules and optimize_params exclusion to scripts/install.sh

The root install.sh was fixed in #23 but scripts/install.sh was missed:
- Add --recurse-submodules to git clone so submodules (e.g. fast_cmaes)
  are available during source builds
- Strip tools/optimize_params from workspace Cargo.toml before building,
  since its path dependencies live outside the repository

Fixes #24

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1836,7 +1836,7 @@ build_from_source() {
   estimate_build_resources
 
   # Clone the repository.
-  local git_args=(clone --depth=1)
+  local git_args=(clone --depth=1 --recurse-submodules)
   if [[ "${RESOLVED_VERSION}" != "latest" ]]; then
     git_args+=(--branch "${RESOLVED_VERSION}")
   fi
@@ -1851,6 +1851,13 @@ build_from_source() {
   fi
   ok "Repository cloned."
 
+  # Remove optional workspace members whose path dependencies (e.g. fast_cmaes)
+  # live outside the repository and are unavailable in a fresh clone.
+  if [[ -f "${source_dir}/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
+    sed -i.bak '/"tools\/optimize_params"/d' "${source_dir}/Cargo.toml"
+    rm -f "${source_dir}/Cargo.toml.bak"
+  fi
+
   # Build the fsfs binary.
   # Unset env vars that would redirect cargo output away from the default
   # target directory.  Without this, users with CARGO_TARGET_DIR or
